name: Bárcena Mayor and Balcón de Cardosa from Correpoco – circular through the Saja-Besaya Natural Park
name_original: Bárcena Mayor y Balcón de Cardosa desde Correpoco – circular por el Parque Natural Saja-Besaya
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Los Tojos traverse a landscape characterized by deep valleys carved by rivers like the Saja and Argoza, alongside extensive oak and beech forests. The region features varied terrain, from lower altitudes to mountain peaks, all within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. These geographical elements create diverse cycling conditions, offering both challenging climbs and scenic routes through woodlands. Best touring cycling routes around Los Tojos The most popular touring cycling route is Bárcena Mayor and Balcón de Cardosa from Correpoco – circular through the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, a 31.2 miles (50.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular journey through the natural park, connecting traditional villages and scenic viewpoints. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Tienda de Pepin – Santa Lucía Recreation Area loop from Cabuérniga, a moderate 27.7 miles (44.6 km) path. This route provides a balanced ride through the region's characteristic Montañés landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Route of The Bridges – Ucieda Saja-Besaya Natural Park, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail leading through the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Los Tojos is defined by deep river valleys, dense oak and beech forests, and traditional Cantabrian villages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Los Tojos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 260 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Tojos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Los Tojos?
A: Los Tojos offers a diverse network of over 20 touring cycling routes. These routes traverse varied terrain within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Los Tojos?
A: The routes in Los Tojos cater to various abilities. While there is 1 easy route and 4 moderate routes, the majority, 17 routes, are classified as difficult. This indicates a region well-suited for cyclists seeking challenging climbs and longer distances.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Los Tojos?
A: You can encounter several unique natural landmarks. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient oak that continued to grow after falling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/994831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lamiña Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; offer a refreshing stop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural feature in the wider area.

Q: Are there any traditional villages or cultural sites to visit on a touring cycling route?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in cultural heritage. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Mayor&lt;/a&gt; is a historic-artistic site, showcasing traditional Montañés architecture with stone houses and cobbled streets. The village of Los Tojos itself also features impressive centuries-old stone arches.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views along the cycling routes?
A: For spectacular panoramic views, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt; or the Mirador La Cardosa, which offers expansive vistas of the Natural Park. Other viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4834775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Tordias&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5635619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Potro Callicedo&lt;/a&gt; also provide excellent scenic stops.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Los Tojos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Los Tojos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.8 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, and the charm of the traditional villages encountered along the way.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Los Tojos?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Los Tojos are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012365563/barcena-mayor-and-balcon-de-cardosa-from-correpoco-circular-through-the-saja-besaya-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Mayor and Balcón de Cardosa from Correpoco&lt;/a&gt; route, which offers a comprehensive tour of the natural park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Los Tojos?
A: While many routes in Los Tojos are challenging, there is 1 easy route and 4 moderate routes that might be more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. Always check the route's specific difficulty and elevation profile before setting out with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Los Tojos?
A: The Saja-Besaya Natural Park, where Los Tojos is located, is generally dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of diverse wildlife like deer, wild boar, and even wolves. Always respect local regulations and wildlife protection guidelines.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on a touring cycling trip in Los Tojos?
A: You'll experience a rich tapestry of landscapes. Expect deep valleys carved by rivers like the Saja and Argoza, extensive oak and beech forests, and varied terrain ranging from lower altitudes to mountain peaks. The region is part of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, ensuring stunning natural beauty throughout your ride.

Q: Are there options for advanced touring cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Los Tojos is an excellent destination for advanced touring cyclists. With 17 routes classified as difficult, you'll find plenty of challenging climbs and significant elevation gains. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38181800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa del Monte de Ucieda – Ermita del Moral loop from Los Tojos&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial distances and elevation for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes suitable for touring cyclists?
A: Yes, the region features notable mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3277001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass&lt;/a&gt; (CA-280) is a scenic route that connects the meseta with the coast, offering stunning views and a rewarding challenge for touring cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Los Tojos?
A: Los Tojos is suitable for outdoor activities throughout the year. However, for touring cycling, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, such as the bellowing of deer in autumn. Summer can be warm, and winter may involve snow at higher altitudes.
tips: Nice vantage point right on the road.

A delightful section to roll through dense beech forests.
Going through it in autumn is to ensure a festival of colors.

Beautiful place especially for hot days since you can cool off in one of them. It is reached by a very cool track that runs next to the Argonza River and then continues through the heart of the Saja-Besaya Park.
One of the most beautiful villages in Spain. And one of the best and cheapest places to eat.
